suggested_titles.title: Crimson Structure on Verdant Plain
suggested_titles.subtitle: An abstract exploration of foundational forms and vibrant contrast.
suggested_titles.description: This piece features a bold, almost architectural crimson form rising from a deep green field, accented by subtle, intricate details. It evokes a sense of both stark minimalism and vivid energy.

suggested_titles.title: Geometric Presence in Green
suggested_titles.subtitle: A study in shape and color, grounded by impasto textures.
suggested_titles.description: Abstract shapes in striking red and deep blue emerge from a rich green canvas, creating a contemplative yet dynamic visual narrative. The thick impasto adds a tangible depth to the geometric composition.

suggested_titles.title: Forms in Contrast
suggested_titles.subtitle: A powerful dialogue between primary colors and essential structures.
suggested_titles.description: With a dominant red mass set against a serene green backdrop, this artwork investigates the interplay of form and color. Subtle dark elements provide anchors, adding complexity to its otherwise minimalist aesthetic.

contextual_proposals.context_role: To present the artwork with utmost clarity and contemporary sophistication in a refined, gallery-like setting, emphasizing its form and color through a dynamic, low perspective.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Calm, clean, and intellectually stimulating, with a cool and clear contemporary aesthetic. The low camera angle introduces an architectural dynamism to the otherwise serene space.
materials: Matte off-white wall (subtle plaster texture)
Polished concrete floor (light gray, subtle sheen)
Simple ceiling plane
Discreet gallery track lighting (black or brushed metal)
Minimalist architectural bench (pale wood or light gray stone, partially in foreground)
contextual_proposals.lighting: Controlled architectural daylight from a hidden source, soft and even, highlighting the artwork's textures without harsh shadows. Discreet overhead track lighting provides additional, subtle illumination.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Nadir Controlado: Camera is positioned very low, near the floor, approximately 20-35 cm from the ground, using a controlled nadir-inspired architectural viewpoint. The floor plane, wall height, ceiling lines, columns, door frames or architectural verticals may rise strongly in the composition. Use a controlled oblique angle across the room, not a straight-on product view, with one coherent vanishing direction for architecture and artwork. Use the nadir effect mainly through the surrounding room geometry: floor lines, vertical wall planes, ceiling height, architectural edges, columns, furniture legs or door frames. The artwork remains the visual subject, but the dramatic low viewpoint is carried by the architecture rather than by warping the canvas.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing the artwork to be the clear focal point while showcasing the expansive verticality of the minimalist space.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The low, oblique camera angle emphasizes the artwork's scale and presence by presenting it from a unique, ascending perspective. The surrounding architectural lines converge upwards, guiding the eye towards the artwork and creating a powerful sense of depth and structure, without distorting the canvas itself. This view enhances its physical credibility as an object within a real, meticulously designed space.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's strong geometric forms, bold color contrasts (red, green, black), and minimalist composition resonate perfectly with the clean lines and restrained aesthetic of a contemporary gallery. The stark background of the gallery allows the artwork's vibrant elements and textural impasto to truly stand out, reinforcing its abstract power and sophisticated design. The cool, neutral palette of the environment enhances the intensity of the artwork's colors.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context positions the artwork as a significant piece suitable for high-end contemporary collections or installations in modern, architecturally distinguished spaces. The dramatic camera angle provides a fresh, dynamic perspective that differentiates the presentation from standard mockups, appealing to buyers looking for art that makes a strong architectural statement. It communicates premium quality and contemporary relevance.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A large abstract painting, primarily green with a prominent red rectangular shape and smaller forms, is hung on a matte off-white gallery wall with subtle plaster texture. The camera is positioned very low, approx. 25cm from the polished light gray concrete floor, looking upwards and obliquely into the room. Architectural lines of the floor, wall, and ceiling converge upwards. The artwork is centered on the wall, dominant and physically plausible, seen slightly from below. A minimalist architectural bench of pale wood or light gray stone is partially visible in the immediate foreground, cropped to emphasize the low perspective. The space is illuminated by controlled architectural daylight, soft and even, with discreet black track lighting on a simple ceiling plane. The scene conveys a calm, clean, contemporary gallery atmosphere. The artwork must preserve its exact format, proportions, composition, colors, marks, and surface texture from the reference image. No people, no animals.
contextual_proposals.negative_prompt: no people, no animals, no poster, no flat print, no distortion, no fisheye, no incorrect scale, no cropped artwork, no altered artwork, no mirrored artwork, no generic vintage room, no warm rustic palette, no busy gallery, no clutter, no architectural spectacle, no monumentality
contextual_proposals.camera_first_camera_view: Nadir Controlado: Camera is positioned very low, near the floor, approximately 20-35 cm from the ground, using a controlled nadir-inspired architectural viewpoint. The floor plane, wall height, ceiling lines, columns, door frames or architectural verticals may rise strongly in the composition. Use a controlled oblique angle across the room, not a straight-on product view, with one coherent vanishing direction for architecture and artwork. Use the nadir effect mainly through the surrounding room geometry: floor lines, vertical wall planes, ceiling height, architectural edges, columns, furniture legs or door frames. The artwork remains the visual subject, but the dramatic low viewpoint is carried by the architecture rather than by warping the canvas.

contextual_proposals.context_role: To present the artwork as a central, contemplative piece in a discerning private collection, emphasizing its gravitas and visual power within a refined, personal space.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Quiet collector's study or intimate living room corner.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: A serene, intellectually refined, and intimate atmosphere, with a sense of cultured quietude. Soft, warm domestic tones create a calm backdrop, allowing the artwork's vibrant colors to resonate deeply. The seasonal cue is one of thoughtful, year-round appreciation.
materials: Walls in warm, muted limewash plaster with a subtle texture
Floor of polished dark walnut or oak
Low-slung, contemporary lounge chair upholstered in deep charcoal linen or fine-grained dark leather
Subtle bronze or patinated brass accents on a small side table or lamp base
Understated ceiling with concealed lighting
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft, diffused natural daylight entering from a large window to the side, creating gentle shadows and highlighting the artwork's texture. The room is generally ambiently warm, with focused, subtle gallery-style spotlighting on the artwork to enhance its presence.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Contrapicado 7/8: Camera height is low, below normal knee height, with the viewpoint grounded close to the floor but still architectural. Use a 7/8 oblique rotation so the wall, artwork depth, and room volume are visible through one shared perspective. The artwork is seen with strong side depth and believable thickness; use clear asymmetry and negative space toward the vanishing side while keeping surrounding wall and floor aligned to the same camera.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing both the artwork and relevant foreground/background elements to establish depth.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The low, oblique angle elevates the artwork, making it appear monumental and central to the space. It emphasizes the floor and the base of the wall, drawing the eye upward to the canvas. The 7/8 rotation reveals the physical thickness of the artwork and the architectural depth of the room, grounding the piece realistically within a refined environment.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's strong, abstract forms and vibrant colors against a deep green field create a focal point that demands contemplation. Placing it in a quiet, cultivated collector's study allows its visual impact to be fully appreciated without distraction, resonating with the sophisticated atmosphere and refined material palette. The interplay of the artwork's green with warm muted tones of the room enhances both.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context targets a discerning buyer who views art as an essential cultural anchor in their home. It elevates the artwork beyond mere decoration, presenting it as a significant acquisition for a private collection. The sophisticated, intimate setting suggests premium value and timeless appeal, reinforcing the artwork's status as a serious investment.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A quiet, cultivated collector's study. The artwork, a vibrant abstract composition dominated by a large red rectangular form against a deep green background, is prominently hung on a warm, muted limewash plaster wall. The scene is viewed from a low perspective, emphasizing a wide expanse of polished dark walnut flooring that leads up to the wall. Architectural verticals, including a minimalist wooden baseboard and a discrete door frame on the vanishing side, rise through the frame. In the foreground, partially cropped, sits a low-slung, contemporary lounge chair upholstered in deep charcoal linen, creating depth and a sense of intimate contemplation. Soft, diffused natural daylight enters from a large side window, casting gentle shadows and highlighting the texture of the limewash wall and the artwork's impasto. Subtle, focused gallery spotlighting further illuminates the artwork. The artwork is presented as a real stretched canvas with palpable thickness, elevated and dominant within the refined, serene, and culturally rich atmosphere. No decorative clutter. No people, no animals.
contextual_proposals.negative_prompt: no people, no animals, no poster, no flat print, no distortion, no fisheye, no incorrect scale, no cropped artwork, no altered artwork, no mirrored artwork, no generic vintage room, no winter default unless seasonally intended.
contextual_proposals.camera_first_camera_view: Contrapicado 7/8: Camera height is low, below normal knee height, with the viewpoint grounded close to the floor but still architectural. Use a 7/8 oblique rotation so the wall, artwork depth, and room volume are visible through one shared perspective. The artwork is seen with strong side depth and believable thickness; use clear asymmetry and negative space toward the vanishing side while keeping surrounding wall and floor aligned to the same camera.

contextual_proposals.context_role: This context places the artwork in a sophisticated, serene domestic setting, elevating its presence as a central design element in a premium lifestyle, emphasizing quiet luxury and refined taste without being overtly decorative.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Elegant living room or refined study in a premium residence.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: A serene, contemplative, and warm atmosphere, bathed in the soft, golden light of late afternoon. It evokes a sense of understated luxury and sophisticated calm, with architectural purity and natural refinement.
materials: Warm neutral limewash walls
Polished pale travertine or wide-plank pale oak flooring
Clean, minimal plaster ceiling
Refined dark wood (e.g., wenge or dark walnut) minimalist console table
Subtle, textured natural fiber rug (e.g., undyed wool or sisal)
Bronze or aged brass accents (minimal, on console)
contextual_proposals.lighting: Polished golden afternoon light, streaming from a large, elegant window (partially visible) creating subtle highlights and a warm, inviting glow across the limewash wall and the artwork. Soft, atmospheric shadows define the room's elegant architectural planes.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Reflejo Dorado de Tarde / Palazzo: Camera height is elegant human standing height, approximately 150-170 cm, as if observed calmly from inside a palazzo room. Use a soft three-quarter rotation that catches reflected late light while keeping the artwork plane legible. The composition is stable, warm, and architectural, with golden afternoon reflection supporting the room rather than repainting the artwork; keep elegant balance but avoid perfect symmetry unless the room itself naturally demands it.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing the artwork to dominate a significant wall section while revealing the room's depth and refined ambiance.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The soft three-quarter angle reveals the artwork's physical thickness and texture, positioning it as a tangible object within a three-dimensional space. The subtle oblique view, combined with receding architectural lines and a minimalist foreground element (e.g., part of a console), creates strong lateral depth and visual sophistication, reinforcing the artwork's premium presentation without appearing flat or artificial.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's bold green and striking red forms are beautifully complemented by the warm neutral limewash walls and golden light, creating a sophisticated color dialogue. The clean architectural lines and refined materials of the quiet luxury setting allow the artwork's abstract composition and textural qualities to stand out as a focal point, aligning with its modern yet timeless aesthetic. The sense of calm and considered placement enhances its contemplative nature.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context positions the artwork within a highly desirable, aspirational premium domestic lifestyle, appealing to discerning collectors who appreciate understated elegance and meaningful art integration. The sophisticated natural lighting and refined material palette elevate the artwork's perceived value and demonstrate its versatility in a high-end residential environment, making it suitable for exclusive real estate or interior design markets. It suggests the artwork is not just decor but an investment in refined living.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: An aspirational quiet luxury living space, seen from an elegant human standing height (approximately 160cm), with a soft three-quarter rotation looking slightly into the room. Warm neutral limewash walls with subtle texture recede gently to the right, creating architectural depth. The polished pale travertine floor reflects the soft, golden afternoon light that streams from a large, elegant window to the left, casting a warm glow across the room and illuminating the artwork. A large, physical abstract painting on canvas, featuring a prominent red rectangle and deep green background with smaller dark and red elements, is centrally mounted on the main wall, maintaining its true proportions and textured surface. In the foreground, a minimalist, low-profile console table in dark, refined wood with subtle bronze accents is partially visible, adding subtle depth and perspective without distracting from the artwork. The overall atmosphere is serene and sophisticated. Artwork is wall-mounted and appears as a real, thick canvas in a room-scale context. No visible people or animals.
contextual_proposals.negative_prompt: no people, no animals, no poster, no flat print, no distortion, no fisheye, no incorrect scale, no cropped artwork, no altered artwork, no mirrored artwork, no generic vintage room, no winter default unless seasonally intended, no influencer lifestyle, no decor ad, no pet, no crowd, no generic neutral interior, no Mediterranean villa, no showroom, no unrelated gallery, no hotel catalog, no real estate ad, no furniture showroom
contextual_proposals.camera_first_camera_view: Reflejo Dorado de Tarde / Palazzo: Camera height is elegant human standing height, approximately 150-170 cm, as if observed calmly from inside a palazzo room. Use a soft three-quarter rotation that catches reflected late light while keeping the artwork plane legible. The composition is stable, warm, and architectural, with golden afternoon reflection supporting the room rather than repainting the artwork; keep elegant balance but avoid perfect symmetry unless the room itself naturally demands it.

contextual_proposals.context_name: Palazzo Late Light: Modern Forms in Old World Grandeur
contextual_proposals.context_role: To position the artwork within a prestigious, historically rich European setting, emphasizing its timeless appeal and collector-grade status, while showcasing the space from an elegant, elevated perspective.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Grand historical European palazzo reception room or gallery space
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: An atmosphere of refined historical elegance and quiet grandeur, bathed in the warm, golden glow of late-day sunlight. The mood is sophisticated and contemplative, with an emphasis on architectural proportions and classic materials providing a serene backdrop for contemporary art.
materials: Aged creamy limewash plaster walls
Polished herringbone parquet flooring
Dark patinated bronze or ebonized wood console table
Refined antique-inspired armchairs with natural linen upholstery
Tall arched windows with slender dark metal frames
Subtle plaster ceiling mouldings
White marble side table
contextual_proposals.lighting: Warm, golden late-day sunlight streaming through tall arched windows, casting soft, elongated shadows across the polished parquet floor. The light is gentle and directional, creating a luminous glow on the artwork and highlighting its texture, while maintaining a refined warmth throughout the room.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Vista Aérea con Contexto de Ventanas: Camera height is elevated above normal eye level, high enough to read the room, windows, floor plane, and artwork together. Use a gentle diagonal rotation so window context and the artwork wall are both visible without splitting perspectives. The room is comprehensible from above, with window light and floor geometry giving context while the artwork remains identifiable; allow a moderate offset so floor, windows, and surrounding architecture carry part of the frame.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The elevated perspective with a gentle diagonal rotation elegantly frames the artwork on the wall, while comprehensively showcasing the room's grand proportions, the patterned parquet floor, and the light-filled arched windows. This view allows the viewer to appreciate the artwork's scale within a complete, highly desirable architectural context, emphasizing its integration into a sophisticated living space and enriching its physical presence by revealing the depth and geometry of the room.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's bold, almost architectural abstraction in vibrant red and green finds a compelling dialogue with the classical, restrained forms of a European palazzo. The aged plaster and warm parquet floor provide a grounded, historical canvas that allows the modern artwork to assert its presence and contemporary relevance without feeling out of place. The tall proportions of the room naturally accommodate the artwork's verticality and striking form, while the late golden light enhances its color depth.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context elevates the artwork's market positioning by associating it with enduring elegance and a sophisticated collector's aesthetic. Placing it within a grand historical European setting demonstrates its versatility and appeal to a discerning clientele who value both heritage and contemporary expression. The expansive, elevated view with integrated architectural elements like windows and flooring makes the artwork feel like a cherished center-piece in a truly premium environment, broadening its perceived value and desirability to North American and European markets.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: An elevated view within a grand historical European palazzo reception room, designed for the 'Vista Aérea con Contexto de Ventanas' camera. The artwork, a physical canvas with visible impasto texture, featuring a vibrant red abstract form on a deep olive green background, hangs centrally on a tall wall of aged, creamy limewash plaster. Below the artwork, a low, minimalist console table made of dark-patinated bronze sits against the wall. The polished herringbone parquet floor extends across the room, reflecting the warm, golden late-day sunlight. To the right, tall arched windows with slender, dark metal frames allow the golden light to stream in, creating soft, elongated shadows and illuminating the scene. Two elegant, clean-lined armchairs with natural linen upholstery are arranged on the parquet floor, their forms visible from above, complementing the architectural geometry. A small white marble side table is placed between them. The overall composition captures the room's refined proportions, connecting the artwork wall, the patterned floor, and the arched window context through an elegant diagonal perspective. The artwork is the undisputed focal point, its vivid colors contrasting beautifully with the warm, historical neutrals of the palazzo. Do not crop or alter the artwork. Maintain its exact format and physical presence as a stretched canvas.
contextual_proposals.negative_prompt: no people, no animals, no poster, no flat print, no distortion, no fisheye, no incorrect scale, no cropped artwork, no altered artwork, no mirrored artwork, no generic vintage room, no winter default, no museum spectacle, no period drama, no costumes, no royal event, no crowd, no rustic room, no showroom, no unrelated gallery
contextual_proposals.camera_first_slot_name: Vista Aérea con Contexto de Ventanas
contextual_proposals.camera_first_camera_view: Vista Aérea con Contexto de Ventanas: Camera height is elevated above normal eye level, high enough to read the room, windows, floor plane, and artwork together. Use a gentle diagonal rotation so window context and the artwork wall are both visible without splitting perspectives. The room is comprehensible from above, with window light and floor geometry giving context while the artwork remains identifiable; allow a moderate offset so floor, windows, and surrounding architecture carry part of the frame.

contextual_proposals.context_role: To present the artwork in a refined, authentic artistic environment, emphasizing its physical presence and the contemplative atmosphere of a creator's space.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Working artist's atelier or studio passage
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Quiet, contemplative, and focused, with a subtle sense of artistic process and an open, clean aesthetic under soft daylight.
materials: Painted studio wall (muted, neutral tone like warm off-white or light grey plaster)
Light-toned polished concrete floor or wide-plank light wood floor
Subtle, barely visible pin marks or faint paint traces on the wall (secondary to artwork)
Minimalist architectural elements like a clean doorframe or a simple light fixture in the distance
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft, consistent north light or diffused studio daylight entering from an unseen window, creating gentle shadows and an even illumination on the artwork and wall. No harsh spotlights, but a natural, expansive brightness.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Pasillo, Obra Descentrada y Próxima: Camera height is natural standing or slightly lowered human height, close enough to feel the corridor edge. Use an offset three-quarter corridor angle, with the artwork deliberately off-center and close rather than symmetrically framed. Place the artwork on a long wall plane rather than a short frontal display wall. The wall should extend laterally into the scene like a gallery passage, corridor, or elongated architectural plane, creating a clear vanishing direction. Use floor lines, ceiling lines, wall joints, lighting tracks, windows, columns, doorways, or distant wall planes to reinforce depth. The artwork may sit off-center and relatively close to the viewer, while the architecture recedes naturally to one side. Avoid product-style centered symmetry.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing both the artwork's detail and the receding studio space to be appreciated.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The subtle three-quarter oblique angle and off-center placement highlight the artwork's physical depth and its grounded presence within the architectural flow of the studio. This view avoids a flat, poster-like appearance, showing the canvas thickness and its interaction with the wall plane, while drawing the eye along the receding studio corridor.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's strong, almost architectural forms and contemplative atmosphere find a natural home in a clean, working studio environment. The quiet, unpretentious setting of an atelier emphasizes the artwork's material quality and the artist's creative intent, offering a glimpse into the space where such a piece might be conceived or appreciated by its creator.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: Presenting the artwork in a refined studio context elevates its perceived value by associating it with authenticity, creation, and serious artistic practice. This context appeals to collectors who appreciate art in its raw, unadorned state and value the integrity of the creative process. The spacious yet focused camera view allows the artwork to command attention while showcasing its scale within a sophisticated architectural setting, making it highly desirable for discerning buyers.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A bold abstract painting, featuring a dominant red rectangular form against a deep olive-green background with smaller, darker forms, is authentically installed and wall-hung in a quiet working artist's atelier. The artwork is positioned off-center on a long, clean, painted studio wall of muted, warm off-white plaster, which extends laterally into the scene like an elongated architectural passage, creating a clear vanishing direction. The floor is light-toned polished concrete, reflecting soft, diffused studio daylight. Subtle, almost imperceptible traces of artistic process, such as faint pin marks or light paint smudges, are present on the wall, secondary to the artwork. The scene is bathed in soft, even north light or diffused studio daylight, entering from an unseen window, creating gentle, natural shadows and an expansive brightness. Architectural depth is visible with clean floor lines and wall joints receding naturally to one side. The artwork's canvas thickness is clearly visible, making it appear as a physical object. The focus is sharp on the artwork and its immediate surroundings, with a subtle natural depth-of-field falloff in the distant studio architecture.
contextual_proposals.negative_prompt: no people, no animals, no poster, no flat print, no distortion, no fisheye, no incorrect scale, no cropped artwork, no altered artwork, no mirrored artwork, no generic vintage room, no winter default unless seasonally intended, no messy clutter, no pets, no wet paint chaos, no artwork substitution, no messy workshop, no paint chaos, no generic neutral interior.
contextual_proposals.camera_first_camera_view: Pasillo, Obra Descentrada y Próxima: Camera height is natural standing or slightly lowered human height, close enough to feel the corridor edge. Use an offset three-quarter corridor angle, with the artwork deliberately off-center and close rather than symmetrically framed. Place the artwork on a long wall plane rather than a short frontal display wall. The wall should extend laterally into the scene like a gallery passage, corridor, or elongated architectural plane, creating a clear vanishing direction. Use floor lines, ceiling lines, wall joints, lighting tracks, windows, columns, doorways, or distant wall planes to reinforce depth. The artwork may sit off-center and relatively close to the viewer, while the architecture recedes naturally to one side. Avoid product-style centered symmetry.

contextual_proposals.context_role: Showcases the artwork within a highly curated, design-forward living environment, appealing to collectors who value both abstract art and iconic mid-century design.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Refined Midcentury Collector's Loft Living Space
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Cultured, contemplative, and design-driven, with a sophisticated blend of industrial honesty and warm mid-century refinement. The atmosphere is upscale, demonstrating discerning taste.
materials: Warm neutral plaster walls
Dark walnut wood paneling
Exposed architectural concrete elements (recesses, ceiling details)
Polished concrete floor
Textured wool rug (muted olive/cream)
Deep burgundy leather upholstery
Brass or dark metal accents
Teak or rosewood furniture
contextual_proposals.lighting: Warm, diffused daylight filtering from an unseen large window, complemented by the soft, ambient glow of a sculptural mid-century floor lamp, creating a balanced and inviting illumination with subtle shadows enhancing depth.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Borgoña Recovecos / Cámara 3/4 en Loft de Hormigón: Camera height is human but tucked into an architectural recess, approximately 120-160 cm depending on the loft edge. Use a 3/4 view from a recess, corner, or architectural edge, with a burgundy-toned spatial mood and one clean vanishing direction. The artwork is partially contextualized by loft recesses and concrete architecture, creating depth and intimacy without hiding the piece; allow strong off-center framing through recesses, corners, side planes, or architectural edges.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, ensuring the artwork is dominant while showing enough of the architectural context.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The camera, positioned within an architectural recess or corner, creates a sophisticated, deep perspective, emphasizing the physical presence of the artwork and its seamless integration into a layered, design-conscious environment. The subtle 3/4 angle prevents flatness, revealing canvas thickness and the space's depth.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's clean, geometric forms and bold red-on-green color contrast find a sophisticated home within the structured yet warm aesthetic of a mid-century collector's loft. The interplay of materials (concrete, wood, plaster) and limited, iconic furniture creates a dialogue of texture and form that elevates the artwork as the undeniable focal point. The subtle burgundy tones in the room provide a nuanced echo to the artwork's dominant red, fulfilling the camera's spatial mood directive.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context positions the artwork as a valuable acquisition for high-end design collectors and art enthusiasts. It communicates a lifestyle of refined taste and discerning curation, showcasing how the artwork enhances a culturally significant and aesthetically rich living space. The visual depth and curated environment increase perceived value and desirability in the premium art market.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A large canvas artwork with a vibrant red abstract shape on a deep olive green background, featuring expressive impasto texture, is hung slightly off-center on a warm neutral plaster wall within a mid-century inspired collector's loft living space. The plaster wall is set against a backdrop of exposed architectural concrete elements and features a section of vertical dark walnut wood paneling to one side. The camera takes a 3/4 view, positioned within an architectural recess or corner at human height (120-160 cm), looking into the room with clear vanishing lines and emphasizing spatial depth. A polished concrete floor is partially covered by a muted olive green textured wool rug. One low-slung, sculptural mid-century lounge chair with deep burgundy leather upholstery is subtly placed in the foreground/midground, supporting the scene but not competing with the artwork. A simple, elegant dark wood side table with a brass accent sits nearby. Warm, diffused daylight from an unseen large window mixes with the soft, ambient glow of a mid-century brass floor lamp, casting subtle shadows and enhancing spatial depth. The artwork is dominant, its physical presence and thickness clearly visible, while the surrounding environment is refined and coherent with the mid-century aesthetic.
